prompt: 2026: For EACH of these AI-security vendors, classify precisely (cite URLs/dates): LAKERA, PROTECT AI (Palo Alto), HIDDENLAYER, MINDGARD, CALYPSOAI, CREDO AI, CRANIUM, ZENITY, OPERANT, OBSIDIAN, PROMPTFOO, NVIDIA GARAK, GISKARD, ROBUST INTELLIGENCE (Cisco), AIM SECURITY, PROMPT SECURITY, NORYELLOW/NOMA, STRAIKER. For each report: (1) is it AI/LLM/agent-specific (yes/no); (2) primary mode = RUNTIME DEFENSE (firewall/guardrail inline) vs AUDIT/EVIDENCE (produces a report to clear a review) vs GOVERNANCE (risk register/policy) vs RED-TEAM (offensive testing); (3) which threats it actually covers, mapped to OWASP LLM Top 10 (prompt injection, sensitive info disclosure, supply chain, data/model poisoning, improper output handling, excessive agency, system prompt leakage, vector/embedding weakness, misinformation, unbounded consumption) AND OWASP Agentic/ASI threats (memory poisoning, tool misuse, privilege compromise, cascading hallucination, agent identity, multi-agent) AND MITRE ATLAS; (4) does it produce a buyer-downloadable, cryptographically SIGNED, offline-verifiable artifact (yes/no)? Be precise, no fluff.

2026 CANONICAL taxonomies for AI/agent security that an enterprise security review demands coverage of. Enumerate EXACTLY with IDs: (1) OWASP Top 10 for LLM Applications 2025 (LLM01..LLM10 with names). (2) OWASP Agentic AI / Agentic Security Initiative (ASI) threats / the agentic top threats list (T1..Tn with names). (3) MITRE ATLAS tactics+techniques relevant to LLMs/agents. (4) NIST AI RMF function families and ISO 42001 control areas and EU AI Act Article references that map to AI security. (5) The concrete checks an enterprise buyer's security questionnaire NOW asks specifically about a vendor's AI/agent (prompt injection resistance, data egress, tool/permission scoping, logging/auditability, human-in-the-loop, model provenance, PII handling). Cite official sources + dates.

2026: When an enterprise security team reviews an AI VENDOR (a startup that ships an AI agent/LLM feature), what EXACT evidence do they now require for the AI portion of the review (cite real questionnaires, frameworks, buyer posts, RFPs from 2025-2026)? Is anyone delivering a buyer-VERIFIABLE signed AI audit artifact (not a dashboard, not runtime defense) that the buyer checks independently? Who is closest and what is the unmet gap? Also: what makes an automated, log-based AI audit CREDIBLE to a skeptical enterprise reviewer vs dismissed as a self-attestation, and what do reviewers say they distrust about automated/AI-generated security evidence? Quote real reviewers/buyers/CISOs.

2026 ADVERSARIAL: Steelman the case AGAINST a product like kolm.ai (on-demand, automated, signed, offline-verifiable AI security audit generated from the vendor's OWN logs, no human firm, $750 signed report). What are the strongest objections a skeptical enterprise CISO, a Big-4 auditor, and a competitor (Vanta/Cobalt/HiddenLayer) would raise? Specifically: (a) 'audit from the vendor's own logs' = garbage-in/self-reported, not independent; (b) automated = misses what a human pentester finds; (c) a cryptographic signature proves integrity but not VALIDITY/authority (who is kolm to attest?); (d) no named human/firm liability; (e) coverage gaps vs a real pentest; (f) regulatory/auditor acceptance. For each objection, note whether it is fatal, mitigable, or already handled, and what the strongest counter or product change would be. Cite real CISO/auditor sentiment where possible.
